div.p: Stack switch on privilege change via TSS

The TSS exists ONLY to provide SS0:ESP0 for ring transitions. Without it, the CPU would push to the user stack — a security disaster (user code controls it). iret reverses the process: pops EIP, CS, EFLAGS, then ESP, SS (detecting CPL change).
• TSS read: ~5 cycles (cached) • Stack switch: ~10 cycles • Push 5 values: ~15 cycles • Total: ~30-50 cycles iret return: ~20-30 cycles

g.text: Only SS0:ESP0 matter for software context switching
